Narrative: On Friday January 10, 2025, I was assigned as the Hackensack High School SRO. I was advised that a student wanted to report a crime and was currently in the main office. Upon arriving to the main office I met the victim, A.R.       As I began to speak with the victim she immediately began to cry. She stated that she was inappropriately touched by the suspect who is also student. The suspect was identified as Manuel Martinez Perez. The victim stated that on Tuesday January 7, 2025  she was in her 9th period class ESL 2 in room 276. She shares this class with the suspect and his cousin Esmeralda Perez Martinez who is a potential witness.       The victim stated during class the suspect gave her a hug. As he was hugging her he dropped his hand down and grabbed her breast over her clothing. She immediately became angry and told him not to touch her. She then advised Esmeralda Perez Martinez that she was touched inappropriately by the suspect. The victim stated that she reported this incident to her her teacher Mrs. Magdalene Symmonds, but she did not understand her due to a language barrier.  The victim reported that this was not the first time she was inappropriately touched by the suspect. She reported that the prior week a similar incident occurred. I notified assistant Principal Celso King of this report.      I spoke to Esmeralda Perez Martinez who may have witness the incident. Perez Martinez stated that she sits in a group with the victim and the suspect. The day of the incident she did not observe the suspect touch the victim because she was busy doing her class work. The witness did confirm that the victim mentioned to her that she was inappropriately touched by the suspect.       The victim's mother, Raquel Lelio, was notified of the incident and responded to the school. She advised me that she was not sure if she wanted to sign criminal complaints against the suspect. She wants to speak with her daughter and husband before making a decision.       Det. E. Reyes was advised of the incident and will follow up with the complainant at a later time.

Narrative: Suspect(s): Unknown.     I responded to the listed location, 90 Prospect Avenue, on a report of a burglary to auto. Upon arrival, I was met by the victim, Kelly Nurnberg-Darzi, who stated on the morning of 01/10/25 she responded to her grey 2006 Honda Pilot and observed all of her work equipment to be missing. The victim advised me she specialized in opening safes and stores several of her high-value tools in the rear of the listed vehicle. The victim stated the listed vehicle was parked in assigned space #20 and did not move it from the assigned space. The victim stated she last recalled observing her tools in the listed vehicle on 01/06/25 and had not returned to the vehicle since. The following items were taken from the listed vehicle:2 Boroscope - $5,000.00 USD8 Redundant Locks - $2,000.00 USD5 Drills - $5,000.00 USD1 large box of drill bits - $1,200.00 USD1 tool bag with several tools - $2,000.00 USD6 Electronic locks - $1,320.00 USD4 FAS locks - $480.00 Total Value - $17,000.00 USD     While on scene, I observed several cameras in the parking garage of 90 Prospect Avenue. I further observed the listed vehicle to have a broken rear passenger side window which gives direct access to the vehicles trunk. The victim stated the damage occurred on 01/04/25 while her son was parking the vehicle. The victim further stated she did not have any time to repair the broken window and remove the tools from the vehicle prior to them being taken.      The victim advised me she believes the suspect may be her husband Uri Darzi whom she is currently in a civil lawsuit with. Sgt. Peralta responded assist.A victim notification form was completed and a copy was given to the victim.

Narrative: On Thursday, January 16, 2025 at approximately 1934 hours, Sgt. Peralta and I responded to 30 Second Street, Apt. L1 on report of a residential burglary. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who reported that she left her apartment at approximately 1200 hours on this date, before returning several hours later, at approximately 1800 hours. The victim further reported that upon returning to her apartment, she observed the exterior door knob to be damaged (loose), as if someone attempted to break in. The victim stated that upon entering the apartment, the interior did not appear to be ransacked, nor did anything appear out of place at this time.      The scene was unable to processed due to the victim contaminating evidence while entering her apartment. The cost of repair for the damaged door knob was approximately $35.00. A Victim Notification was completed and the victim was provided with a copy upon our departure. There were no known suspects at the time this report was taken. Det./Sgt. Tripodi was notified.

All_Incident_Types: Motor Vehicle Theft, Recovered Stolen Vehicle
Narrative: Suspect(s): Unknown     The victim, Kimberly Trujillo, responded to Police Headquarters on a report of a Motor Vehicle Theft.  Upon arrival, the victim stated that her 2022 grey 4-door Honda Accord bearing NJ reg: L17PUM had been stolen from her apartment complex's parking lot and had been located in Rochelle Park. The victim further stated the listed vehicle was parked in assigned space number #2 and was last known to be parked there at approximately 2300 hours. The victim advised me she was in possession of the master key and no other party had access to her vehicle. The victim further advised me the spare key to her vehicle was misplaced approximately two (2) years in the past. Both license plates were attached to the vehicle at the time the victim parked the vehicle.     The victim stated on 02/17/2025 at approximately 0800, she received a phone call from Rochelle Park Police Department Det. Galina #151 advising her the listed vehicle had been located behind a CrossFit gym in Rochelle Park. Det. Gallina #151 further advised the victim, that the listed vehicle was located with all four (4) wheels removed from the vehicle. The victim further stated upon receiving the phone call, they responded to their second vehicle which was also parked in their apartment complex's parking lot. At this time, the victim observed an E-Z Pass and Camera from the stolen vehicle left on the hood of her second vehicle.      The victim stated she would like to have the vehicle processed should it be recovered and signed a consent to search form. A victim notification form was completed and a copy was given to the victim. The vehicle's insurance information was unable to be provided due to the paperwork being inside the glove box of the stolen vehicle.The vehicle registration was given to dispatch and was entered into NCIC. A stolen vehicle entry was made into the Auto Theft Task Force website.The vehicle's estimated value is approximately $22,000 USD.  See Rochelle Park Case #2025-002492.

Narrative: On February 17, 2025 I responded to 300 Atlantic Street (HUMC Parking Garage) on a report of a motor vehicle theft. Upon arrival, I met with the victim who is the registered owner of the vehicle and his wife. The victim advised me that his wife parked the vehicle (2020 Honda Accord blue) inside the monthly permit parking section near the entrance of the garage at approximately 2000 hours last night. This morning around 0900 hours they arrived to the garage and discovered that the vehicle was missing. They advised me that they only have one key and checked the area for the vehicle but were met with negative results. I advised Headquarters to check if the vehicle was towed from the premises which also yielded negative results. At this time I advised the registered owner the procedure with reporting a stolen vehicle. At this time he stated he wanted the vehicle processed and signed a consent to search form. A victim notification form was also completed and a copy was provided to the victim. Upon completion the vehicle was placed into NCIC and ATTF as stolen.
